mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.81% as of 2008-09-01 indicates aggressive easing cycle
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle following financial shock
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.1% as of 2008-09-01 reflects rising unemployment from financial crisis
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in less than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window during recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in retail sales (RSAFS) combined with falling housing starts (HOUST) signals collapsing consumer demand and construction activity, indicating recession risk that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors price in earnings compression.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST falls below 900k units annualized
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 895k units and RSAFS at 375.5B as of 2008-09-01 reflect housing crisis impact
regime_hint: demand shock from financial crisis and housing collapse
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (joint signal) produces lower Rank IC than either series alone over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below the 12-month trend signals weakening manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side contraction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors anticipate earnings misses from cyclical sectors.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 110.3 (vintage) vs 98.1 (revised) as of 2008-08-01 shows significant downward revision indicating supply weakness
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in less than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals accelerating labor market deterioration that reduces household income and consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as consumer discretionary earnings decline.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 100k month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137.3M (vintage) vs 136.8M (revised) and UNRATE at 6.1% as of 2008-09-01
regime_hint: synchronized labor market shock during financial crisis
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in less than 58% of triggered instances, or the joint signal produces lower predictive power than UNRATE alone over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in real GDP growth (GDPC1) below 1% annualized signals recession entry that compresses equity risk premiums and earnings multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors reduce growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate falls below 1% annualized over prior quarter
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11727.4B (vintage) as of 2008-04-01 reflects pre-crisis peak before sharp contraction
regime_hint: recession regime with negative GDP growth
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in less than 56% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window during recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: A widening spread between inflation (CPIAUCSL) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals negative real rates that incentivize equity investment as a hedge against currency debasement, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors shift from cash to equities.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 12-month change > FEDFUNDS by more than 2 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 218.88 and FEDFUNDS at 1.81% as of 2008-09-01 creates negative real rate environment
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 58% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal produces lower Rank IC than FEDFUNDS alone over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts (HOUST) below 800k units annualized signals housing market collapse that reduces construction employment and consumer wealth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors anticipate financial sector stress and reduced economic activity.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 895k units (vintage) and 844k units (revised) as of 2008-08-01 reflects housing crisis severity
regime_hint: housing market collapse during financial crisis
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in less than 54% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window when HOUST < 800k.
